lever_summary: Arena/scratch reuse for batched Householder QR. qr_decompose_matrix allocated per matrix: r=matrix.to_vec(), a Vec<Vec<f64>> reflector store (k inner Vecs), tau, and both Q/R output buffers (~6 allocs/matrix x batch). Now all working buffers live in a QrScratch reused across the whole vmapped batch; zero per-matrix allocation. Householder arithmetic unchanged.
method: Same-worker A/B normalized by vmap_eigh (untouched by this change) as a worker-speed calibration; candidate and stashed-baseline ran in separate RCH Criterion processes on different-speed workers, normalized via the in-process qr/eigh ratio.

change.lever: Added QrScratch {r, v_store, tau_store, q_out, r_out}; qr_decompose_matrix now takes &mut QrScratch, clears+reuses each buffer and writes Q/R into q_out/r_out; batch_qr_multi allocates one QrScratch before the batch loop and extends the output element Vecs from q_out/r_out.

isomorphism.arithmetic: identical Householder reflections, order, threshold (v_norm_sq > EPSILON*1e4), tau, R/Q updates, and diagonal-sign normalization; only allocation strategy changed
isomorphism.zero_reflector_branch: the <=threshold branch zeroes the reused reflector exactly as the original stored a fresh zero vector; tau==0 makes the Q loop skip it either way

validation.name: qr_and_full_suite
validation.result: 256 + 55 passed incl all test_batch_trace_qr_multi_* (assert exact Q/R values)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: 1.46x (worker-corrected) is below a clean 2x but is a bit-exact arena/slab structural change (addendum-endorsed) on a top-3 dispatch cost with ICE Score 7.5, making batched QR allocation-free. batch_svd_multi (vmap_svd 169us, #2 cost) has the identical per-slice allocation pattern (svd_decompose_matrix) — filed as the ready follow-on; QR+SVD together address ~306us of the profile.
